applied_optimizations.name: torch.compile with mode=default
applied_optimizations.description: Applied torch.compile(mode='default') to the model. This fuses BatchNorm and AvgPool operations into efficient Triton kernels internally, eliminating intermediate memory writes and reducing kernel launch overhead. Per amd-kernel-optimization skill docs, this is the recommended first approach for ROCm.

attempted_but_failed.name: Custom Triton fused BN+AvgPool kernel
attempted_but_failed.reason: Correctness issues - output mismatch despite careful index calculation. Debugging would require significant time.

attempted_but_failed.name: Single AvgPool3d(kernel_size=4) instead of two 2x2x2
attempted_but_failed.reason: Slower performance (4.30ms vs 3.73ms) - torch.compile's fusion is better optimized for separate operations

attempted_but_failed.name: channels_last_3d memory format
attempted_but_failed.reason: No significant improvement - torch.compile already optimizes memory layout

notes: torch.compile(mode='default') provides 2.53x speedup by fusing BatchNorm+AvgPool into Triton kernels
Main bottleneck is ConvTranspose3d (MIOpen) at 29.3% - already well-optimized by AMD's library
Data transposes (39.3% combined) are layout conversions - hard to eliminate without full kernel fusion
Custom Triton kernel approach had correctness issues that would require significant debugging time
The current solution follows skill docs recommendation: torch.compile FIRST for ROCm

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_4
attempted_but_failed.reason: Third consecutive trial with no output. The agent is likely attempting overly complex approaches and timing out. Need to force a simple, incremental approach starting from the known-working torch.comp

attempted_but_failed.name: trial_5
attempted_but_failed.reason: Fourth consecutive trial with no output. Agent is timing out or hitting silent errors. Must force an extremely minimal, copy-paste-ready approach starting from the known-working torch.compile solution

global_notes: For this problem, torch.compile(mode='default') is the optimal practical approach
Writing custom Triton kernels for ConvTranspose3d would be extremely complex and unlikely to beat MIOpen
The fused BatchNorm+AvgPool Triton kernels generated by torch.compile are already efficient
Further optimization would require debugging the custom fused kernel indexing or exploring aiter libraries
